rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 30, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant B.R. Funsten & Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed September 28. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DORIS HENDERSON et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 30, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Baldwin Contracting Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a) and 1010.6, CRC 2.251 and G.O. 158, which applies pursuant to the asbestos Case Management Order. Defendant¿s proof of service shows that defendant electronically served its motion on August 10, 2012. G.O. 158 explicitly states that the filing and service provisions of C.C.P. section 1010.6 shall apply. With a noticed hearing date of October 25, 2012, defendant failed to provide proper notice by failing to extend the notice period by two court days as required by C.C.P. 1010.6. Plaintiffs argued that defendant failed to provide proper notice so they did not waive such objection by also substantively opposing defendant¿s motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LISHA WILSON VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 30, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Clayton Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issue 2 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Wilson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Peter Mason¿s deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND TOWNSEND et al VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 30, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose defendant¿s demurrer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court.
